items.label: Volume Max V1 [cc]
items.notes: Reference cylinder displacement fixed at 1000 cc. (Selectable in Engineering Model.)

items.label: Compression Ratio
items.notes: Compression ratio. Higher CR increases end-of-compression pressure and thermodynamic severity. Reference operating range: 20–100.

items.label: Temperature Input [°C]
items.notes: Intake temperature at start of compression. Fixed at 27 °C in the Reference Model baseline.

items.label: Lambda
items.notes: Equivalence ratio governing combustion heat release and peak temperature estimation.

items.label: Volume Min V2 [cc]
items.notes: Minimum cylinder volume at TDC, derived from V1 and CR.

items.label: Loss Unburned [%]
items.notes: Fraction of fuel energy not converted to useful heat due to incomplete combustion. Reference value: 1%.

items.label: Loss Coolant Net [%]
items.notes: Fraction of combustion heat rejected to coolant before internal recovery. Fixed at 10% in the Reference Model.

items.label: Mechanical efficiency [%]
items.notes: Mechanical efficiency converting indicated work to brake work. Reference value: 90%.

items.label: Bore to Stroke Ratio
items.notes: Geometric ratio influencing piston area, force transmission, and mean piston speed characteristics.

items.label: Combustion phasing (ATDC)θ  [Deg]
items.notes: Combustion phasing (ATDC). Fixed at 5° in the Reference Model configuration.

items.label: RPM
items.notes: Engine speed. Influences brake power, torque trends, and BSFC characteristics.

items.label: Water Phase
items.notes: Water phase state under compression constraint (No Evaporation / Evaporates), evaluated against pressure–temperature boundary conditions.

items.label: Intake Pressure P1 (derived) [bar]
items.notes: Derived intake pressure schedule as a function of CR to maintain the defined “No Evaporation” compression envelope in the Reference Model.

items.label: Mass Water [g]
items.notes: Water mass per cycle determined to satisfy the sub-cooled compression constraint within the Reference Model envelope. Final water control strategy is defined and calibrated in the Engineering Model.

items.label: Water Fuel Ratio [%]
items.notes: Water-to-fuel mass ratio operating window: 300–1000% (3×–10× fuel mass). This broad envelope supports thermodynamic exploration within the “No Evaporation” compression constraint. Final ratio selection and control are calibrated in the Engineering Model.

items.label: Compression Exponent n_comp
items.notes: Compression exponent window n = 1.05–1.20, representing near-isothermal compression behaviour within the Reference Model operating envelope. Final effective exponent is governed by calibrated control strategy in the Engineering Model.

items.label: End of Compression Temp T2 [°C]
items.notes: End-of-compression temperature (T2). Primary indicator of thermal severity and knock margin.

items.label: End of Compression Pressure P2 [bar]
items.notes: End-of-compression pressure (P2). Evaluated against the sub-cooled compression constraint.

items.label: Work Compression [J]
items.notes: Compression work per cycle.

items.label: Compression Energy Fraction(CEF) [%]
items.notes: Compression Energy Fraction. Portion of total heat input represented by compression work.

items.label: Compression/Expansion Work Ratio (Wcomp / Wexp) [%]
items.notes: Compression-to-expansion work ratio. Indicator of compression burden relative to expansion recovery.

items.label: Peak Combustion Pressure P3 (Ideal) [bar]
items.notes: Peak combustion pressure assuming ideal heat release and no losses.

items.label: Peak Combustion Pressure P3 (Real) [bar]
items.notes: Peak combustion pressure after thermal loss adjustments.

items.label: Peak Piston Force (F_max) [kN]
items.notes: Force scales with piston area; geometry changes load even at same pressure.

items.label: Pressure Exhaust [bar]
items.notes: Exhaust pressure at end of expansion (real cycle condition).

items.label: Peak Combustion Temp T3 (Ideal) [°C]
items.notes: Peak combustion temperature assuming no thermal or mechanical losses.

items.label: Peak Combustion Temp T3 (Real) [°C]
items.notes: Peak combustion temperature after accounting for coolant and unburned losses.

items.label: Temperature Exhaust [°C]
items.notes: Exhaust temperature at end of expansion (real cycle).

items.label: Heat in [J]
items.notes: Heat input per cycle calculated from fuel energy and equivalence ratio.

items.label: Loss Exhaust [J]
items.notes: Exhaust heat component after energy balance resolution.

items.label: Heat Unburned [J]
items.notes: Unburned fuel energy loss.

items.label: Heat Friction [J]
items.notes: Mechanical friction loss expressed as work equivalent.

items.label: Cooling Heat (Gross) [J]
items.notes: Gross cooling heat rejected for material and thermal protection prior to recovery.

items.label: Recovered Heat (IHRL) [J]
items.notes: Cooling heat recovered through the Internal Heat Recovery Loop (IHRL) and returned as useful sensible energy.

items.label: Cooling Heat (Net) [J]
items.notes: Net cooling loss after recovery:Q_cool_net = Q_cool_gross − Q_rec_IHRL.

items.label: Latent Energy Fraction [%]
items.notes: Latent Energy Fraction during expansion. Represents portion of cycle energy associated with phase-change contribution in expansion.

items.label: Effective Expansion Ratio (EER)
items.notes: Effective Expansion Ratio determined by combustion phasing (θ_ATDC), indicating how efficiently post-combustion pressure is converted into useful expansion work and reduced exhaust loss. CR ≠ EER

items.label: Expansion Work (Ideal) [J]
items.notes: Ideal expansion work per cycle.

items.label: Expansion Work (Real) [J]
items.notes: Expansion work after thermal losses.

items.label: Work Brake [J]
items.notes: Brake work per cycle after mechanical efficiency.

items.label: Net Work (Ideal) [J]
items.notes: Net indicated work (ideal cycle).

items.label: Net Work(Real) [J]
items.notes: Net indicated work after coolant and combustion inefficiency losses.

items.label: Thermal Efficiency (Ideal) [%]
items.notes: Ideal indicated thermal efficiency.

items.label: Thermal Efficiency (Real) [%]
items.notes: Real indicated thermal efficiency including thermal losses.

items.label: Efficiency Brake [%]
items.notes: Brake thermal efficiency including mechanical losses.

items.label: IMEP (Ideal) [bar]
items.notes: Ideal indicated mean effective pressure.

items.label: IMEP (Real)  [bar]
items.notes: Brake indicated mean effective pressure

items.label: BMEP [bar]
items.notes: Brake Mean Effective Pressure.

items.label: Torque [Nm]
items.notes: Brake torque output.

items.label: Brake Power [KW]
items.notes: Brake power at selected engine speed.

items.label: Mean Piston Speed [mps]
items.notes: Mean piston speed, function of stroke and rpm.

items.label: BSFC g/kWh [g/kWh]
items.notes: Brake Specific Fuel Consumption (g/kWh).

items.label: CO₂ Intensity  [g/kWh]
items.notes: CO₂ intensity per unit brake energy delivered (g/kWh).
